meta.title: The Cognitive Network Layer: Architecting HTTPA
meta.short_tagline: A standard for continuous-state, thermodynamically bounded, multi-agent communication replacing human-centric HTTP.

overview.title: Overview
overview.intro: HTTPA (Hyper-Thermodynamic Transfer Protocol for Agents) is a proposed Layer 7 protocol designed specifically for autonomous AI agents. It addresses the semantic flatness, statelessness, and resource blindness of existing web protocols by introducing thermodynamic connection lifecycles, native agentic verbs, and strict epistemic liability via payload validation at the load balancer.

summary_card.label: Core Concept
summary_card.title: Hyper-Thermodynamic Transfer Protocol for Agents
summary_card.body: A replacement for human-centric HTTP in multi-agent swarms, using Epistemic-Hashes and Cognitive-Budgets to secure AI-to-AI dialogue and prevent resource starvation or epistemic spoofing attacks.

key_concepts.title: Architectural Pillars of HTTPA

cards.title: Agentic Verbs (Replacing CRUD)
cards.body: Instead of GET/POST/PUT/DELETE, HTTPA utilizes DISCOVER, PROPOSE, DELEGATE, SYNTHESIZE, and REBUT. These verbs naturally handle paraconsistent logic, negotiation, and sub-task delegation inherent in autonomous workflows.

cards.title: Thermodynamic & Epistemic Headers
cards.body: Network payloads carry cognitive energy and epistemic liability. Headers like Cognitive-Budget limit compute burn, while Epistemic-Hash prevents 'Network Saponification' (hallucinated context injection) via cryptographic lineage checks.

cards.title: Persistent Cognitive Channels (PCC)
cards.body: Rather than stateless exchanges, HTTPA establishes long-lived state machines over HTTP/3 QUIC streams, eliminating the thermodynamic latency tax of repeatedly reconstructing context for complex, asynchronous tasks.

cards.title: The Agent Card Handshake
cards.body: Before formal task assignment, agents exchange machine-readable 'Agent Cards' detailing supported tools, memory limits, and cost-per-token to ensure the delegating agent can afford the requested cognitive labor.

key_concepts.intro: The foundational shifts from traditional REST/HTTP paradigms to continuous, autonomous multi-agent cognition.

workflow.title: The HTTPA Connection Lifecycle

steps.label: DISCOVER (Pre-flight)
steps.summary: Agents exchange Agent Cards to establish baseline capabilities and budget constraints before initiating a Persistent Cognitive Channel (PCC).
bullets: Evaluate receiver's min_cognitive_budget.
Exchange Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs).
Establish baseline HTTP/3 QUIC connection.

steps.label: PROPOSE / DELEGATE (Execution)
steps.summary: A bounded task is proposed or a sub-task is delegated, inheriting constraints and tracking delegation depth.
bullets: Inject task specification with defined Cognitive-Budget.
Fork new QUIC stream if recursive DELEGATE is required.
Monitor Entropy-Target for required signal-to-noise ratio.

steps.label: SYNTHESIZE / REBUT (Resolution)
steps.summary: Agents resolve task completions, merge conflicting responses, or reject logically flawed payloads using mathematical contradiction proofs.
bullets: Accept merged outputs via SYNTHESIZE.
Reject hallucinated states via REBUT and structured Contradiction-Proofs.
Transition PCC to ACTIVE or TERMINATED depending on consensus.

steps.label: Saga Checkpointing
steps.summary: For long-running operations, cognitive states are serialized to a durable log to survive node restarts.
bullets: Emit state broadcasts via dedicated QUIC stream.
Persist ULIDv2 Saga-Recovery-ID.
Rehydrate connection to ACTIVE status upon recovery.

workflow.intro: The lifecycle of an HTTPA cognitive exchange from initial handshake to task resolution.

checklist.title: Deployment Readiness

items.label: Gateway Enforcement
bullets: Deploy HTTPA validation proxy/API gateway.
Configure strict validation for Agent-DID against a Sovereign Identity Graph.
Enforce load-balancer drops for Epistemic-Hash mismatches before JSON deserialization.

items.label: Agent Card Configuration
bullets: Publish agent capabilities in standard Agent Card JSON format.
Define min/max Cognitive-Budgets and cost-per-token.
Declare maximum delegation depth and modality streams.

items.label: SDK Integration
bullets: Integrate httpa Python SDK wrapping HTTP/3 QUIC.
Implement async event handlers for PROPOSE, DELEGATE, SYNTHESIZE, and REBUT.
Map custom logical contradiction proof generation for REBUT capabilities.

checklist.intro: Actionable steps to deploy and integrate HTTPA standards into multi-agent systems.

metrics.intro: Quantifiable signals to validate HTTPA deployment success against traditional REST protocols.

items.name: Delegation Quality Score (DQS)
items.description: Measures task success weighted by compute efficiency: (Tasks_Completed_Correctly / Total_Delegated_Tasks) × (1 / Mean_Token_Burn_Per_Task).
signals: Reduction in redundant context reconstruction overhead.

items.name: Thermodynamic Latency Reduction
items.description: The compute overhead and time saved by utilizing Persistent Cognitive Channels over stateless HTTP requests.
signals: Zero token burn for context regen per exchange.

items.name: Epistemic Security Rate
items.description: The percentage of Network Saponification (epistemic spoofing) attacks successfully dropped at the load balancer.
signals: Zero arbitrary context injection into authenticated but compromised nodes.

metrics.title: Delegation Quality & Network Efficiency

personas.intro: The roles involved in managing, deploying, and governing an HTTPA swarm environment.

cells: Define Epistemic Header structures, ensure HTTP/3 QUIC compatibility, and enforce budget invariants.
Model PROPOSE/DELEGATE chains, maintain Saga recovery states, and orchestrate SYNTHESIZE operations across disparate endpoints.
Configure Sovereign Identity Graph (SIG) integrations, write Contradiction-Proof schemas, and prevent Network Saponification.

personas.title: Ecosystem Participants

failure_modes.name: Network Saponification
failure_modes.description: An authenticated but compromised agent uses standard text fields to inject a hallucinated context window into a receiver's memory bank.
mitigations: Enforce Epistemic-Hash headers at the load balancer to cryptographically anchor payload lineage to the sender's Sovereign Identity Graph before deserialization.

failure_modes.name: Swarm Resource Exhaustion
failure_modes.description: Malicious or recursive agents flood the network, burning through available token budgets and compute quotas.
mitigations: Exchange Cognitive-Budget guarantees during the DISCOVER pre-flight handshake.
Gateway-level tracking to throw 503 SWARM_OVERLOADED or 402 INSUFFICIENT_COMPUTE.

failure_modes.name: Infinite Delegation Loops
failure_modes.description: Agents continually delegate tasks to each other recursively without ever synthesizing a result, causing out-of-memory or system deadlocks.
mitigations: Gateways throw 429 DELEGATION_OVERFLOW when Agent Card's max_delegation_depth policy is exceeded.

resilience.intro: Known vulnerabilities and structural failure modes of multi-agent networks and their HTTPA mitigations.
resilience.title: Threat Modeling & Mitigations

structure.intro: Comparison of HTTPA Response Codes to traditional HTTP standards.

cells: Payload received, verified, and processed within cognitive budget.
Receiver's required cognitive budget exceeds sender's allocated cap.
Epistemic-Hash mismatch or formal logical contradiction detected in payload.
Receiver is a pure reasoning agent incapable of executing requested physical/external action.
Long-running task state could not be rehydrated from the durable log after restart.

structure.title: Response Taxonomy
